concept.title: What is markdown-validator?
concept.shortdesc: markdown-validator is a rule-based tool that checks your Markdown files against editorial standards defined by your team. It catches missing metadata, incorrect front-matter values, and structural problems before your content reaches review or publication.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Learn what markdown-validator does, why documentation teams use it, and what kinds of problems it catches automatically.

conbody.note: Applies to: markdown-validator 0.2.x (last updated 2026-03-20). Planned changes in v0.3 (negation operator) and v0.4 (structured DOM) may affect rule behaviour described here.
Pull request just blocked? Go to Validate a single file to reproduce the failure locally, then read the validation report to fix it. You do not need to read this topic first.

section.title: What it does
section.p: Your team defines rules once in a JSON file. The tool applies them to every article and reports exactly which rules each file passes or fails — catching missing fields, stale dates, wrong values, and structural issues that reviewers would otherwise flag manually.
markdown-validator checks two parts of each file:

row.entry: Required fields present, values match allowed values, date is recent, naming conventions
Document body — content below the front matter
Exactly one H1, required H2 sections present, heading capitalization, no empty sections

section.title: How you use it

ul.li: Locally before a pull request — run the tool from the command line to catch and fix problems before reviewers see them.
Automatically in CI/CD — the pipeline validates every pull request. Required failures block the merge.

section.p: Your content lead maintains the rule set. You run the validator and fix what it reports.

section.title: What you need

ul.li: The rule set file path (ask your content lead; typically rules/tutorial.json in the repository)

section.p: To get started: Install markdown-validator | How validation works
